academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a minimum three-year bachelor's degree or equivalent in Historical and Geographical studies or in related humanities and social-science fields relevant to Mobility Studies.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent).
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: The official course page lists Historical and Geographical studies and related subjects such as Philology, Literature, Foreign Languages, Sociology, Anthropology, Global Studies, and Social and Political sciences.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: Country-based specific entry title and GPA requirements apply. The official course page does not publish a programme-specific minimum GPA threshold [uncertain].

accepted_proofs: TOEFL iBT with a minimum score of 80.
IELTS Academic or General Training with a minimum score of 6.0.
Cambridge English B2 First with a minimum score of 173.
Pearson Test of English Academic with a minimum score of 65.
Trinity College London ISE II or another certificate listed by the University of Padua.
Only certificates obtained no more than five years before the start of the selection process are accepted.
exemption_rules: No certificate is required if the applicant obtained the entry qualification by completing previous education entirely in English.
No certificate is required for native English speakers from the countries listed by the University of Padua.
language_requirements.deadline: For open-access degree programmes, the university requires the language certificate by 31 August 2026.

application_deadline_eu.notes: Call Three is only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.

application_deadline_non_eu.call_two: Not available to non-EU students residing outside Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.call_three: Not available to non-EU students residing outside Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Only Call One applies to non-EU students residing outside Italy; Call Three is restricted to EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.

required_documents.notes: Listed among the standard documents for master's degree applications with an international qualification.
Mobility Studies gives particular importance to the motivation letter, which must be specifically tailored to the programme.
Required for master's applications; if it is not issued in English or Italian, an official translation is needed.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's degree diploma or enrolment/provisional certificate
required_documents.notes: If the final qualification has not yet been awarded, an enrolment or provisional certificate is accepted.
EU or equivalent applicants can use an ID card; other applicants use a passport.
Required only for non-EU citizens already residing in Italy.

required_documents.document_name: English language certificate or exempting proof
required_documents.notes: Required if the applicant does not qualify for a published exemption.

required_documents.document_name: Official translations into English or Italian for academic documents issued in other languages
required_documents.notes: The official course page states that official translations are needed when academic documents are not in English or Italian.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's diploma translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: The enrolment page states that previous qualifications must be translated and legalised for enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of records translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: Required at enrolment together with the previous degree documentation.
Required for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy.
Non-EU students residing outside Italy must complete pre-enrolment through Universitaly.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No written entrance test; possible interview via the application platform
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Applicants invited by the selection committee during the assessment procedure.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: Interview dates are not published on the official course page [uncertain].
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: No score threshold is published; the official page only states that the committee may invite applicants to an interview.

source_quotes.quote: Class LM-84 - Historical Sciences - Mobility Studies
A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent)
the selection committee reserves the right to invite applicants to take an interview

uncertainty_notes: Mobility Studies is an English-taught study track within the master's degree in Historical Sciences, so programme identity was consolidated from the official department page and the official apply.unipd.it course page.
The task-provided URL https://www.unipd.it/en/mobility-studies currently redirects to the University home page, so it was not used as the main programme-page source.
The official open-access course page does not publish a programme-specific minimum GPA threshold or interview calendar.
